D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

The Airy Disc of a Circular Aperture

diffract light through a round hole and find the central disc and faint rings

A round aperture concentrates most light into a central disc whose first dark ring sits at sinθ = 1.22 λ/D. The pattern follows the Bessel function J₁.

Set the aperture diameter and wavelength. The left half renders the Airy pattern; the right plots its radial intensity with the first dark ring marked in gold.
